actually_means_en: A mild exclamation of exasperation, like "come on!"
usage_note_en: Safe around grandparents. Use when someone is being slow.

actually_means_en: An affectionate tease for a lovable troublemaker
usage_note_en: Sounds harsh, isn't. Said with a smile, usually to a friend who just pulled a prank.
Universal filler curse. Works for stubbed toes and slow wifi alike.

actually_means_en: A cheeky brat, usually a kid or a young upstart
usage_note_en: Teasing, not vicious. Aimed at someone acting younger than they are.

actually_means_en: "You idiot" — calling someone out for a dumb move
usage_note_en: Standard-issue insult. Fine among friends, risky with strangers.
Reserve for actual bad decisions, like cutting you off in traffic.
The all-purpose reaction to bad news. Interchangeable with dropping your phone.
No subtlety here. Understood instantly, everywhere.

actually_means_en: A scumbag, someone who behaved disgustingly
usage_note_en: For someone who genuinely crossed a line, not just annoying.

actually_means_en: Used as "damn it!" or "what the hell"
usage_note_en: Extremely common exclamation, more punctuation than insult.

actually_means_en: "Screw it" — resigned acceptance of a bad situation
usage_note_en: The national shrug. Deploy when nothing can be done.
The strongest common exclamation. Save it for real disasters.
The German default curse. Safe in front of coworkers.

actually_means_en: An affectionate "you rascal" for someone who just pulled a stunt
usage_note_en: Sounds like an insult, lands like a hug. Said with a laugh.
Playground-level insult. Fine between friends.
Common Austrian/German staple for everyday clumsiness.
Extremely common. Barely counts as swearing.
The all-purpose reaction. Stub your toe, say this.
Stronger than trottel, still survivable at a family dinner.
Understood instantly across every language, basically.
The classic. Deploy after real betrayal, not minor annoyance.

actually_means_en: A scumbag, someone who did something disgusting
usage_note_en: Reserve for genuinely gross behavior.
Strong. Only for people who truly deserve it.
A command, not a description. Say it and walk away.
